research_lens_metadata.paper: Bag of Dims: Training-Free Mechanistic Interpretability via Dimension-Level Sign Patterns

original_problem_statement: Reading features from transformer hidden states requires training a separate model: sparse autoencoders need millions of contextual activations and GPU-hours; probes need labeled datasets per property.

reconstructed_problem.what_the_field_thinks: Transformer hidden states encode features in learned, distributed directions. Decoding requires learned rotations (SAEs, probes) to find the right basis. The superposition hypothesis suggests features are geometrically packed.
reconstructed_problem.what_this_paper_shows: Individual dimensions of the standard basis function as independent binary registers. Sign encodes content, magnitude encodes confidence. Features are subsets of dims with consistent sign patterns—readable by counting sign agreements with zero training.
reconstructed_problem.key_insight: The sign/magnitude decomposition is new: prior work reads activation magnitudes through learned projections; this work reads signs in the standard basis directly.
hidden_assumptions_exposed: Superposition may not bind in real transformers (MI < 0.006 bits vs 0.05-0.10 in toy models)
Features need not be orthogonal directions; they can be sign agreement patterns over shared dims
The standard basis is already a feature basis
Per-dim independence is an emergent property of training
SAE-scale coverage is achievable without optimization
reconstructed_problem.paradigm_shift: The open problem shifts from 'finding the right rotation' (via SAEs/probes) to 'cataloging what each dimension encodes at each layer'.

key_techniques: Single-token type cache: one forward pass per vocab token, no context, no labels
Per-dimension AUC for feature discovery (sign separability)
Hamming distance/sign agreement counting as training-free classifier
Unsupervised discovery via centered signs + nearest-neighbor Hamming
Causal sign-flip intervention during live forward pass
Static weight inspection for FFN→residual circuit tracing
what_was_not_needed: No gradient computation for feature reading
No contextual data for feature discovery
control_conditions: Null calibration with 100 random anchor sets
Disjoint coalition control for causal intervention

key_questions_raised.question: Why do different training objectives all produce the same per-dim structure?
key_questions_raised.suggested_answer: Shared architecture (residual stream + FFN writes + sparse activation).

key_questions_raised.question: Does the structure persist at 70B+ parameters?
key_questions_raised.suggested_answer: Consistent from 4B to 32B suggests yes, but untested.

key_questions_raised.question: Can we induce concepts (not just suppress)?
key_questions_raised.suggested_answer: Requires a magnitude operation. Not provided in this paper.

key_questions_raised.question: What do the flipping 42% of dims encode?
key_questions_raised.suggested_answer: Likely syntactic role, reference, context-dependent properties.

key_questions_raised.question: Does D/V ratio fully explain cross-model variation?
key_questions_raised.suggested_answer: Primary variable identified; other factors may contribute.

what_would_a_skeptic_say: Sign patterns may be correlational epiphenomena
Causal effect only near output—limits the 'computational unit' claim
Qwen3-32B polysemy failure suggests framework breaks at low D/V ratios
1500 unsupervised features may not be semantically coherent
what_makes_it_compelling: Random-weight controls rule out architectural artifacts
Sign/magnitude decomposition explains the logit lens
Cross-modal universality shows structure is fundamental to transformer training
